title: Create new board and move / copy Item to new created board
excerpt:
How can i make the following automatisation? 
when status changed to approved, create new board from Template “x” and move / copy Item to this new created board

posts.author: a.mooser (Alexander)
posts.cookedHtml:
How can i make the following automatisation?

when status changed to approved, create new board from Template “x” and move / copy Item to this new created board

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @a.mooser!

Transparently there is no super seamless way to set this up natively, however I did have an idea that might help in some way.

You would need to have an additional status column set up in your board which essentially will be the trigger for the item to move to the new board - that said, each time the new board is created you’d need to create a new status label that aligns with the board name.

From the outset, you’d set up this recipe (which I believe you already have setup):

Screen Shot 2023-03-16 at 4.52.42 pm1244×592 25.1 KB

Then, once that board is created, you’d then want to go back into the first board and create the following recipe using the secondary status column:

Screen Shot 2023-03-16 at 4.55.29 pm1304×644 29.1 KB

I completely understand that this isn’t the most ideal process, however if you aren’t looking to implement any third party applications this might be the best way forward

posts.author: a.mooser (Alexander)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you Bianca,

unfortunately that is not what i am exactely looking for.

Let me describe my usecase:

Having a Board for Project Requests a new Project gets a Project Number once it is Approved at the Project Request Board. Additional there is a Set of Data captured while Project Request.

Once the Project is the accepted and gets Status Approved a New Board (New Project) will be created from a Template and Board gets the new Name (Project Number + Title).

Now it comes: Together with this Creation of the Board also the Project Number & Data Captured while Request should be copied automatically to the new Board.

I guess ideal would be to have the following and add : and create Item in this NEW board

So far i tryed the workaround to copy this into the Template which then will be in the new Project Board.

image1230×427 3.39 KB

But issue then is, that this Items will be added and added and added…

posts.author: a.mooser (Alexander)
posts.cookedHtml:
All,

i finally found a solution for this Case and want to share with you.

Usecase:
New Project listed as one Line (Item) in the Project & Portfolio Approval Board.
With approval a new Item will be created (with all project Request Data) at the right Project Board Template.

image703×257 3.67 KB

Then within the Template Board following Automatization was installed.
When item is Created then create new board from this template and delete the item…to finally be clean and fresch for the next Project to be created.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @a.mooser !

Firstly, I apologise for my delay in responding, I completely missed your follow up! That said, you have come up with a brilliant solution/workaround Thank you for sharing as this certainly will be valuable to other community members!

posts.author: derek.greer (Derek)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, this is EXACTLY what I need to do. you did all this with out of the box templates or had to get an app?

posts.author: a.mooser (Alexander)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Derek,

indeed out of the box. But as described only possible through the additional step of copying first into the template board. Then create new Board from this template boarad.

posts.author: abacusblackburn (Abe Blackburn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sad update to this thread:

The new template editor essentially breaks this really creative workaround. They have hard-locked a ‘publish changes’ button for new templates, meaning that you would have to manually approve the new item in the template, rendering this automation useless.

Support

The Template Editor

3 min read Feature   Who can use this feature: All users Available on the Standard plan and above    We're excited to announce the launch of our new Template Editor. With this new fe...

My workaround has been to repurpose an old template from prior to the template editor launch (May 2024 onwards) for this workflow - but if you don’t have that option, this workaround won’t work for you.

Monday.com team, we really need the option to:

  • when status changes
  • create board from template
  • and move item to THAT NEW BOARD

Or even just the option to link to the new board in a status update, or some way of linking to the new board.

posts.author: Leah1 (Leah)
posts.cookedHtml: @abacusblackburn Great point and I have seen this as well. This feature would be very helpful to my team. Have you submitted a feature request for this? I’d vote on it!
